candidates.title: Reviewer validation command guide refresh
candidates.summary: Refresh reviewer walkthroughs, fix stale safety-doc version references, repair broken internal links, and link the reviewer golden path from the README and external walkthrough.
candidates.user_value: Reviewers can navigate from README to deterministic validation commands without hitting stale or broken references.
candidates.safety_boundary: No runtime code; only docs and link/reference hardening.
candidates.ranking_reason: Closes visible reviewer-journey gaps surfaced by the README cleanup; low risk.

candidates.title: Dashboard/report UX polish after v0.6.9
candidates.summary: Fix dashboard status-badge mapping for schema statuses, add the HTML safety banner to Markdown dashboard output, improve empty/redacted diagnostics rendering, and sort backtest runs deterministically by timestamp.
candidates.user_value: Report-quality output and static dashboard exports are more useful and less misleading for reviewers.
candidates.safety_boundary: Dashboard remains read-only, local, and has no execution controls or external assets.
candidates.ranking_reason: Small, high-visibility render-only polish that extends v0.6.9 report-quality work.

candidates.title: Backtest report schema checker ergonomics
candidates.summary: Improve scripts/check_backtest_report_schema.py with --json, per-status counts (valid/invalid/legacy/unreadable), --fail-on-legacy, a final summary, and direct unit tests.
candidates.user_value: Reviewers and CI get machine-readable, deterministic schema validation diagnostics.
candidates.safety_boundary: Still local-only read-only validation; no execution or broker/provider calls.
tests_checks: New unit tests for JSON output, exit codes, legacy handling
candidates.ranking_reason: Purely additive checker UX; aligns with the v0.6.9 validation theme.

candidates.title: Template source-of-truth simplification
candidates.summary: Make src/atlas_agent/templates/routine-trader/ the single canonical source, delete the byte-identical root-level duplicate, and update checkers/tests/docs to reference the packaged copy.
candidates.user_value: Removes a long-standing drift hazard and eliminates duplicate template maintenance.
candidates.safety_boundary: Must preserve fail-closed defaults in .env.example, risk.example.yaml, and brokers.example.yaml; no execution changes.
tests_checks: atlas init smoke from clean checkout
candidates.ranking_reason: Packaged copy is canonical; root duplicate removed; workspace fallback uses packaged filesystem path.

candidates.title: Code inventory follow-up import/API classification
candidates.summary: Refresh docs/development/code-inventory-followups.md and tests/test_code_inventory_imports.py to remove integrated modules, re-verify deferred imports, and improve API-classification clarity.
candidates.user_value: Keeps the code inventory accurate and useful for reviewers.
candidates.safety_boundary: Docs/test only; no runtime behavior changes.

candidates.title: Release-assurance metadata cleanup
candidates.summary: Remove stale hardcoded v0.5.x-era copy-paste from scripts/release_assurance.py, archive old tracked artifacts/release_assurance/v0.5.9* packs, and tighten .gitignore for generated local evidence.
candidates.user_value: Generated local evidence and release-assurance output stay accurate and free of stale version claims.
candidates.safety_boundary: Only affects generated local evidence; no runtime trading behavior changes.
candidates.ranking_reason: Touches tracked historical evidence and generated artifact hygiene; isolate to avoid accidental data loss.

candidates.title: Public docs link/reference hardening after README cleanup
candidates.summary: Fix broken CHANGELOG links to v0.5.8 rc notes, refresh stale capability-inventory headers, convert backticked doc paths to markdown links, and extend the public-docs consistency scan list to cover reviewer-facing docs added since v0.6.8.
candidates.user_value: Public docs stay internally consistent and trustworthy after the README streamlining.
candidates.safety_boundary: Docs/checker only; no runtime or safety-boundary changes.
candidates.ranking_reason: Direct follow-up to the README cleanup; low risk and reviewer-facing.

deferred.title: Release-assurance metadata cleanup
deferred.reason: Touches tracked historical evidence; defer to a focused generated-artifact hygiene batch.

safety_boundaries: Live trading remains disabled by default.
Live submit remains disabled by default.
Provider execution remains disabled by default.
Broker execution remains disabled by default.
Risk gates remain deterministic and unchanged.
Human approval remains required for live order flow.
Credentials are not loaded unless explicitly configured.
No provider APIs, broker APIs, or real webhooks are called from CI or local checks.
constraints: No tag, GitHub release, or PyPI publish yet
release_criteria: dev_check, ci_check, and release_check --quick pass locally
CI on main is green after each batch is pushed
Version is bumped to 0.6.10 in pyproject.toml and src/atlas_agent/__init__.py
scripts/check_v0610_release_prep.py and matching tests are created (release-prep mode)
Owner approval is obtained for tag cutover
